heading: Presidential awards for teaching excellence
label: 1881b Presidential awards for teaching excellence
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Science Foundation Authorization Act of 1988, and not as part of the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: The President is authorized to make Presidential Awards for Excellence in Mathematics and Science Teaching to kindergarten through grade 12 school teachers of mathematics and science who have demonstrated outstanding teaching ability in the field of teaching mathematics or science.

children.label: B
children.text: Each year the President is authorized to make no fewer than 110 awards under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: from each of the several States;

children.label: III
children.text: from the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;

children.label: IV
children.text: from schools established outside the several States and the District of Columbia by any agency of the Federal Government for dependents of the employees of such agency; and

children.label: I
children.text: from the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands;

children.label: III
children.text: from the Virgin Islands of the United States; and
The President shall carry out this subsection, including the establishment of the selection procedures, after consultation with the Director and other appropriate officials of Federal agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: Funds to carry out this subsection for any fiscal year shall be made available from amounts appropriated pursuant to annual authorization of appropriations for the Foundation for Education and Human Resources.

children.label: B
children.text: Amounts made available pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be available for making awards under this subsection, for administrative expenses, for necessary travel by teachers selected under this subsection, and for special activities related to carrying out this subsection.
